# Report for 2026-02-12 (Thursday, February 12th, 2026)

14 different users commented on 37 different issues.

## Recommended Actions

 * Response Recommended
    * @JSRossiter reported differences in import sorting and formatting in [microsoft/TypeScript-go#2444](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444#issuecomment-3892747174)
    * @virtuallyunknown asked whether auto-imports were generated by tsgo or another method in [microsoft/TypeScript-go#2444](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444#issuecomment-3892869810)
    * @JSRossiter provided requested setup information in [microsoft/TypeScript-go#2444](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444#issuecomment-3893352536)
    * @neo773 provided repro steps with a repository link in [microsoft/TypeScript-go#2780](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2780#issuecomment-3896509428)

## Activity Summary

### [Issue microsoft/TypeScript-go#2444](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444) (Open, `Domain: Editor`)

**Missing code actions \(\`editor\.codeActionsOnSave\`\) with \`typescript\.experimental\.useTsgo\` in VSCode**

*Enabling typescript.experimental.useTsgo in VSCode disables most code actions on save, preventing missing imports from being added automatically.*

 * (3 days ago) **virtuallyunknown** closed the issue
 * (3 days ago) **virtuallyunknown** reopened the issue
 * [3 days ago](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444#issuecomment-3874988236) **virtuallyunknown** reported that settings worked as expected and listed available code actions, noted testing details, and apologized for accidentally closing the issue
 * [today](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444#issuecomment-3892747174) **JSRossiter** reported differences in import sorting and multiline import formatting in the new organize imports feature
 * [today](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444#issuecomment-3892869810) **virtuallyunknown** asked whether auto-imports were generated by tsgo or another method after noticing separate runtime and type imports on different lines
 * [today](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444#issuecomment-3892892365) **JSRossiter** explained that tsgo didn’t support auto imports yet, so they were still using the old extension, and that their import style was enforced by eslint-plugin-import’s consistent-type-specifier-style rule
 * [today](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444#issuecomment-3892978400) **virtuallyunknown** explained that they had removed the VSCode extension and were using the old language server without auto-imports, and noted that the typescript.experimental.useTsgo setting wasn’t grayed out when the extension was missing
 * [today](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444#issuecomment-3893285702) **iisaduan** cced @johnfav03 for ordering differences and import combining issues and asked @virtuallyunknown to clarify old/expected behavior and import syntax
 * [today](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444#issuecomment-3893291423) **iisaduan** said "@JSRossiter the 2 space indent issue is something I'm currently looking into. Can you share your settings.json?"
 * [today](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444#issuecomment-3893352536) **JSRossiter** indicated they were using Cursor and had installed the extension via a downloaded VSIX, sharing their JSON configuration
 * [today](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444#issuecomment-3893421933) **iisaduan** said "Very helpful, thank you!"
 * [today](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2444#issuecomment-3895108787) **virtuallyunknown** explained that initial import example reflected old behavior due to a removed extension and advocated separating type imports into distinct statements for readability

### [Issue microsoft/TypeScript-go#2703](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2703) (Closed, `bug`, `Domain: JSX`, **ahejlsberg**)

**JSX render prop children incorrectly intersects with \`{}\`**

*ts-go erroneously intersects JSX render prop children with {} leading to assignment errors absent in TypeScript 6.0.*

 * (3 days ago) **DanielRosenwasser** added labels `bug`, `Domain: JSX`, and assigned to **ahejlsberg**
 * [today](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2703#issuecomment-3891984715) **ahejlsberg** explained that PR #1149 revealed an escaped anyFunctionType being inferred as {} for the children property and proposed adding logic to map it to a regular empty object type
 * (today) **ahejlsberg** closed the issue

### [Issue microsoft/TypeScript-go#2778](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2778) (Open)

**\[ServerErrors\]\[TypeScript\] linux\-x64\-7\.0\.0\-dev\.20260212\.1 vs **

*The linux-x64-7.0.0-dev.20260212.1 TypeScript error-delta pipeline run experienced 158 timeouts, five git clone failures, and one unknown failure while analyzing 300 popular repositories.*

 * created by **typescript-bot**
 * [today](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2778#issuecomment-3894721917) **typescript-bot** reported a runtime panic in textDocument/codeAction due to slice bounds out of range and provided a stack trace

 * [today](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2778#issuecomment-3894721967) **typescript-bot** reported a panic in textDocument/completion due to a token cache mismatch
 * [today](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2778#issuecomment-3894721994) **typescript-bot** reported a panic handling request for textDocument/completion due to a nil pointer dereference

### [PR microsoft/TypeScript-go#2779](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/pull/2779) (Closed, **jakebailey**, **Copilot**)

**No\-op: TS PR \#61746 already ported \(eager project reference map initialization\)**

*Go codebase already implements TypeScript PR #61746’s eager project reference map initialization, so no lazy initialization patterns remain.*

 * created by **Copilot**
 * (today) **Copilot** assigned to **Copilot**, **jakebailey**
 * (today) **jakebailey** closed the issue

### [Issue microsoft/TypeScript-go#2780](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2780) (Open, `Domain: Editor`, **andrewbranch**)

**High memory consumption in a large pnpm monorepo**

*PR #2502 causes minor edits in a large pnpm monorepo to spike the TypeScript extension memory from ~700MB to ~35GB.*

 * created by **shinichy**
 * **shinichy** added label `Domain: Editor`
 * **jakebailey** assigned to **andrewbranch**
 * [later](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2780#issuecomment-3896509428) **neo773** reproduced the issue on their NestJS monorepo, noting memory usage spiked to ~25-30GB and shared a repository link

### [Issue microsoft/TypeScript-go#2781](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript-go/issues/2781) (Open)

**resolving library types with import assignment \`import = require\(\)\` fails**

*Named and default type exports from @sendgrid/mail included via import assignment work in TypeScript 6.0 but tsgo reports missing members and default-only import errors.*

 * created by **ayZagen**
